records.subject: flavescence dorée
records.object: The affected vines must be removed and destroyed. If infection occurs, steps are taken to control the species of leafhopper that is spreading the disease. Alternatives to the use of insecticides are being sought,” recalls Agroscope de Changins, which, in a press release, announces that it has succeeded in deciphering the genetic heritage of the pathogen. The disease is caused by phytoplasmas, bacteria that lack a cell wall. To be able to eradicate this disease, it is important to understand how the pathogen spreads and what happens to the affected plant. This is why Agroscope researchers have deciphered the genetic heritage of the pathogen Candidatus phytoplasma vitis . “Unlike
